entries.name: Museo de La Plata
entries.desc: Argentine natural sciences museum famous for its paleontology halls, with mounted dinosaur skeletons and fossils of South America's extinct Ice Age megafauna.

photo.alt: Museo de La Plata building facade in La Plata, Argentina
photo.credit: <!-- photo: la-plata-museum-building.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "La Plata Museum Facade" by Leandro Kibisz (Loco085) · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:La_Plata_Museum_Facade.JPG -->

entries.name: Australian Museum
entries.desc: Australia's oldest museum, focused on natural history and Indigenous cultures, with collections of native wildlife, minerals, and First Nations artifacts.

photo.alt: Australian Museum building facade in Sydney
photo.credit: <!-- photo: australian-museum-building.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "AustralianMuseum" by J Bar at English Wikipedia · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:AustralianMuseum.jpg -->

entries.name: Naturhistorisches Museum Wien
entries.desc: Vienna's grand natural history museum, home to the prehistoric Venus of Willendorf figurine, meteorite collections, and extensive zoological and mineral halls.

photo.alt: Naturhistorisches Museum Wien exterior in Vienna
photo.credit: <!-- photo: naturhistorisches-museum-wien.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.5 · "NaturhistorischesMuseumWien01" by böhringer friedrich · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:NaturhistorischesMuseumWien01.jpg -->

entries.name: Royal Tyrrell Museum of Palaeontology
entries.desc: Museum devoted entirely to paleontology, set in the fossil-rich badlands of Alberta, with one of the world's largest displays of mounted dinosaur skeletons.

photo.alt: Royal Tyrrell Museum exterior set in the Alberta badlands
photo.credit: <!-- photo: royal-tyrrell-museum.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Royal Tyrrell Museum" by Carolyn Miaral · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Royal_Tyrrell_Museum.JPG -->

meta: One of World's Largest Dinosaur Displays

entries.name: Canadian Museum of History
entries.desc: Canada's most-visited museum, across the river from Ottawa, tracing the country's human history, with a Grand Hall of Pacific Northwest totem poles and First Peoples exhibits.

photo.alt: Canadian Museum of History building in Gatineau, Quebec
photo.credit: <!-- photo: canadian-museum-of-history.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "The Canadian Museum of Civilization" by Michel Rathwell from Cornwall, Canada · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:The_Canadian_Museum_of_Civilization_(39094456960).jpg -->

entries.name: National Museum of China
entries.desc: One of the world's most-visited museums, on the east side of Tiananmen Square, chronicling Chinese history and art from ancient bronzes through the modern era.

photo.alt: National Museum of China building on Tiananmen Square, Beijing
photo.credit: <!-- photo: national-museum-of-china.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "NationalMuseumofChinapic1" by Nagyman · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:NationalMuseumofChinapic1.jpg -->

entries.name: Shanghai Museum
entries.desc: Museum of ancient Chinese art, particularly noted for its collections of bronzes, ceramics, calligraphy, and jade, housed in a distinctive round-topped building.

photo.alt: Shanghai Museum's round-topped building on People's Square
photo.credit: <!-- photo: shanghai-museum.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "Shanghai Museum on People's Square" by Alexey Yakovlev · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Shanghai_Museum_on_People%27s_Square.jpg -->

entries.name: Terracotta Army Museum
entries.desc: Site museum preserving the buried army of life-size clay soldiers made to guard China's first emperor, displayed in situ within the excavation pits where they were found.

photo.alt: Rows of terracotta warriors in the excavation pit near Xi'an
photo.credit: <!-- photo: terracotta-army-pit-1.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "Qin Shihuang Terracotta Warriors, Pit 1" by Gary Todd from Xinzheng, China · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Qin_Shihuang_Terracotta_Warriors,_Pit_1_(26738789015).jpg -->

entries.name: Grand Egyptian Museum
entries.desc: Vast archaeological museum near the Giza pyramids, built to display the complete Tutankhamun collection together for the first time along with monumental statuary.

photo.alt: Grand Egyptian Museum exterior near the Giza pyramids
photo.credit: <!-- photo: grand-egyptian-museum.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public Domain · "Grand Egyptian Museum 2025 (57645).jpg" by Amr F.Nagy · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Grand_Egyptian_Museum_2025_(57645).jpg -->

entries.name: Muséum national d'Histoire naturelle
entries.desc: France's national natural history institution centered on the Jardin des Plantes, best known for its Grande Galerie de l'Évolution and paleontology galleries.

photo.alt: Grande Galerie de l'Évolution facade, Muséum national d'Histoire naturelle, Paris
photo.credit: <!-- photo: museum-national-histoire-naturelle.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Grande galerie de l'évolution, Paris 10 April 2014.jpg" by Ken and Nyetta · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Grande_galerie_de_l%27%C3%A9volution,_Paris_10_April_2014.jpg -->

entries.name: Musée du quai Branly – Jacques Chirac
entries.desc: Museum devoted to the indigenous arts and cultures of Africa, Asia, Oceania, and the Americas, in a Jean Nouvel building beside the Seine near the Eiffel Tower.

photo.alt: Musée du quai Branly – Jacques Chirac building facade, Paris
photo.credit: <!-- photo: musee-du-quai-branly.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "Musee du quai branly.JPG" by Captainxandre · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Musee_du_quai_branly.JPG -->

entries.name: Cité des Sciences et de l'Industrie
entries.desc: Large science and technology museum in the Parc de la Villette, with interactive exhibits, a planetarium, and the spherical La Géode cinema on its grounds.

photo.alt: Cité des Sciences et de l'Industrie building, Parc de La Villette, Paris
photo.credit: <!-- photo: cite-des-sciences-et-de-l-industrie.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Cité des Sciences et de l'Industrie @ Parc de La Villette @ Paris" by Guilhem Vellut · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cit%C3%A9_des_Sciences_et_de_l%27Industrie_@_Parc_de_La_Villette_@_Paris_(28853466272).jpg -->

entries.name: Pergamon Museum
entries.desc: Museum Island institution known for monumental reconstructed antiquities, including the Ishtar Gate of Babylon and the Market Gate of Miletus.

photo.alt: Reconstructed Ishtar Gate of Babylon, Pergamon Museum, Berlin
photo.credit: <!-- photo: ishtar-gate-pergamon-museum.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "The Ishtar Gate of Babylon at the Pergamon Museum, Berlin, Germany. From Iraq, 6th century BCE.jpg" by Osama Shukir Muhammed Amin FRCP(Glasg) · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:The_Ishtar_Gate_of_Babylon_at_the_Pergamon_Museum,_Berlin,_Germany._From_Iraq,_6th_century_BCE.jpg -->

entries.name: Museum für Naturkunde
entries.desc: Berlin natural history museum whose centerpiece is a mounted Brachiosaurus, one of the tallest dinosaur skeletons on display, alongside a preserved Archaeopteryx fossil.

photo.alt: Museum für Naturkunde main building, Berlin
photo.credit: <!-- photo: museum-fur-naturkunde-berlin.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Berlin Naturkundemuseum 2.jpg" by Rüdiger · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Berlin_Naturkundemuseum_2.jpg -->

entries.name: Deutsches Museum
entries.desc: One of the world's largest science and technology museums, set on an island in the Isar, covering mining, aviation, shipping, physics, and astronomy.

photo.alt: Deutsches Museum building on the Isar, Munich
photo.credit: <!-- photo: deutsches-museum-munich.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Deutsches Museum Muenchen-11.JPG" by AHert · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Deutsches_Museum_Muenchen-11.JPG -->

entries.name: Acropolis Museum
entries.desc: Modern museum at the foot of the Acropolis housing sculptures and artifacts recovered from the site, with a top-floor gallery aligned to the Parthenon.

photo.alt: Acropolis Museum glass walkway over excavated ruins, Athens
photo.credit: <!-- photo: acropolis-museum-athens.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "New Acropolis Museum 5.jpg" by Tomisti · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:New_Acropolis_Museum_5.jpg -->

entries.name: National Archaeological Museum
entries.desc: Greece's largest archaeological museum, spanning prehistory to late antiquity, with the Mask of Agamemnon, the Antikythera mechanism, and Cycladic figurines.

photo.alt: National Archaeological Museum of Athens building exterior
photo.credit: <!-- photo: national-archaeological-museum-athens.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "National Archaeological Museum of Athens.jpg" by Srishti Sethi · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:National_Archaeological_Museum_of_Athens.jpg -->

entries.name: Indian Museum
entries.desc: The oldest and largest museum in India, with wide-ranging collections of antiquities, sculpture, fossils, and art, including Buddhist relics and a Bharhut railing.

photo.alt: Indian Museum's colonnaded courtyard and facade, Kolkata
photo.credit: <!-- photo: indian-museum-kolkata-courtyard.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "Indian Museum - Kolkata 2012-11-16 2095.JPG" by Biswarup Ganguly · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Indian_Museum_-_Kolkata_2012-11-16_2095.JPG -->

entries.name: National Museum
entries.desc: India's premier national museum, covering the subcontinent from the Indus Valley civilization onward, with sculpture, miniature paintings, and decorative arts.

photo.alt: National Museum building facade with flags, New Delhi
photo.credit: <!-- photo: national-museum-new-delhi.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "India national museum 01.jpg" by Miya.m · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:India_national_museum_01.jpg -->

entries.name: Israel Museum
entries.desc: Israel's national museum of art and archaeology, whose Shrine of the Book displays the Dead Sea Scrolls beside a large-scale model of Second Temple-era Jerusalem.

photo.alt: Shrine of the Book's white dome, Israel Museum, Jerusalem
photo.credit: <!-- photo: shrine-of-the-book-jerusalem.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "20110225 Israel 0412 Jerusalem (5539903749).jpg" by Dan Lundberg · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:20110225_Israel_0412_Jerusalem_(5539903749).jpg -->

entries.name: Museo Egizio
entries.desc: Museum dedicated exclusively to ancient Egyptian art and archaeology, holding one of the largest such collections in the world outside Cairo.

photo.alt: Museo Egizio facade on Via Accademia delle Scienze, Turin
photo.credit: <!-- photo: museo-egizio-turin.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Egyptian Museum, Turin (1).jpg" by Prof. Mortel · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Egyptian_Museum,_Turin_(1).jpg -->

entries.name: Tokyo National Museum
entries.desc: Japan's oldest and largest museum, in Ueno Park, holding the country's foremost collection of Japanese art and antiquities, including samurai swords and Buddhist sculpture.

photo.alt: Honkan, the main building of the Tokyo National Museum, Ueno Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: tokyo-national-museum-honkan.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Museo nazionale di tokyo, honkan.JPG" by sailko · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Museo_nazionale_di_tokyo,_honkan.JPG -->

entries.name: Nairobi National Museum
entries.desc: Kenya's flagship museum covering natural history, culture, and art, notable for its collection of early hominid fossils from the Rift Valley and East African wildlife exhibits.

photo.alt: Nairobi National Museum entrance signage
photo.credit: <!-- photo: nairobi-national-museum-entrance.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Nairobi National Museum 01.JPG" by Ruslik0 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Nairobi_National_Museum_01.JPG -->

entries.name: Museo Nacional de Antropología
entries.desc: Mexico's national anthropology museum in Chapultepec Park, renowned for Mesoamerican artifacts such as the Aztec Sun Stone and monumental Olmec heads.

photo.alt: Front facade of the Museo Nacional de Antropología, Mexico City
photo.credit: <!-- photo: museo-nacional-de-antropologia.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Frontis del edificio del Museo Nacional de Antropología.JPG" by Carlos yo · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Frontis_del_edificio_del_Museo_Nacional_de_Antropolog%C3%ADa.JPG -->

entries.name: Rijksmuseum van Oudheden
entries.desc: The Netherlands' national museum of antiquities, holding Egyptian, Classical, and Near Eastern collections, including the ancient Egyptian Temple of Taffeh indoors.

photo.alt: Rijksmuseum van Oudheden exterior, Leiden
photo.credit: <!-- photo: rijksmuseum-van-oudheden-leiden.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Rijksmuseum van Oudheden Leiden.jpg" by Rijksmuseum van Oudheden · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Rijksmuseum_van_Oudheden_Leiden.jpg -->

entries.name: Museo Larco
entries.desc: Private museum in a colonial-era mansion showcasing pre-Columbian art, especially Moche pottery, gold and silver ornaments, and a noted erotic ceramics gallery.

photo.alt: Colonial-era mansion housing Museo Larco, Lima
photo.credit: <!-- photo: museo-larco-colonial-mansion.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Lima museo larco.jpg" by Velvet · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Lima_museo_larco.jpg -->

entries.name: State Historical Museum
entries.desc: Russia's national history museum in a distinctive red building on Red Square, covering the nation from prehistoric times through the Romanov dynasty.

photo.alt: State Historical Museum's red facade on Red Square, Moscow
photo.credit: <!-- photo: state-historical-museum-moscow.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "State Historical Museum, Moscow, Russia.jpg" by Andrey · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:State_Historical_Museum,_Moscow,_Russia.jpg -->

entries.name: National Museum of Korea
entries.desc: Korea's flagship museum of history and art, tracing the peninsula from prehistory onward, with celadon ceramics, Buddhist statuary, and the Pensive Bodhisattva.

photo.alt: National Museum of Korea exterior, Seoul
photo.credit: <!-- photo: national-museum-of-korea-seoul.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Seoul-National.Museum.of.Korea-01.jpg" by Ian Muttoo · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Seoul-National.Museum.of.Korea-01.jpg -->

entries.name: Museo Nacional de Ciencias Naturales
entries.desc: Spain's national natural sciences museum, with collections spanning zoology, geology, and paleontology, including skeletons of extinct South American mammals.

photo.alt: Palacio de las Artes e Industrias, home of Madrid's natural sciences museum
photo.credit: <!-- photo: palacio-de-las-artes-e-industrias-madrid.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Palacio de las Artes e Industrias (Madrid) 01.jpg" by Luis García (Zaqarbal) · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Palacio_de_las_Artes_e_Industrias_(Madrid)_01.jpg -->

entries.name: National Palace Museum
entries.desc: Museum holding the former Chinese imperial collection, spanning millennia of art, with treasures such as the Jadeite Cabbage and the Meat-shaped Stone.

photo.alt: National Palace Museum complex on its hillside, Taipei
photo.credit: <!-- photo: national-palace-museum-taipei.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "NationalPalace MuseumFrontView.jpg" by Peellden · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:NationalPalace_MuseumFrontView.jpg -->

entries.name: Topkapi Palace Museum
entries.desc: Former principal residence of the Ottoman sultans, now a museum of imperial treasures, sacred relics, manuscripts, and the palace harem overlooking the Bosphorus.

photo.alt: Gate of Salutation entrance to Topkapi Palace, Istanbul
photo.credit: <!-- photo: topkapi-palace-gate-of-salutation.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Topkapi Palace (Topkapı Sarayı), Istanbul, Turkey (9603571659).jpg" by l0da_ralta · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Topkapi_Palace_(Topkap%C4%B1_Saray%C4%B1),_Istanbul,_Turkey_(9603571659).jpg -->

entries.name: National Museum of Scotland
entries.desc: Wide-ranging national museum covering Scottish history, natural sciences, world cultures, and technology, including Dolly the cloned sheep among its exhibits.

photo.alt: Sandstone-clad Museum of Scotland building, Edinburgh
photo.credit: <!-- photo: museum-of-scotland-edinburgh.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Museum of Scotland.jpg" by Maccoinnich~commonswiki · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Museum_of_Scotland.jpg -->

entries.name: British Museum
entries.desc: Encyclopedic museum of human history and culture, holding the Rosetta Stone, the Parthenon sculptures, Egyptian mummies, and Assyrian reliefs across its collections.

photo.alt: British Museum facade, London
photo.credit: <!-- photo: british-museum.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "British Museum Facade" by Ludi Ling · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:British_Museum_Facade.JPG -->

entries.name: Natural History Museum
entries.desc: London natural history collection set in a landmark Romanesque building in South Kensington, famous for its dinosaur gallery, whale skeleton, and mineral halls.

photo.alt: Natural History Museum, London
photo.credit: <!-- photo: natural-history-museum-london.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "The Natural History Museum, London - DSCF0401" by Rept0n1x · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:The_Natural_History_Museum,_London_-_DSCF0401.JPG -->

entries.name: Science Museum
entries.desc: South Kensington museum of science, technology, and medicine, with exhibits on the Industrial Revolution, spaceflight, and computing including Stephenson's Rocket.

photo.alt: Science Museum, Exhibition Road, London
photo.credit: <!-- photo: science-museum-london.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Science Museum, Exhibition Road (cropped)" by Shadowssettle · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Science_Museum,_Exhibition_Road_(cropped).jpg -->

entries.name: Victoria and Albert Museum
entries.desc: The world's largest museum of applied and decorative arts and design, spanning ceramics, textiles, fashion, sculpture, and metalwork across many cultures and centuries.

photo.alt: Victoria and Albert Museum entrance, London
photo.credit: <!-- photo: victoria-and-albert-museum.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Victoria & Albert Museum Entrance, London, UK - Diliff" by Diliff · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Victoria_%26_Albert_Museum_Entrance,_London,_UK_-_Diliff.jpg -->

entries.name: Imperial War Museum
entries.desc: Museum of modern conflict and its impact on society, covering the two World Wars onward, with military hardware, personal accounts, and a Holocaust gallery.

photo.alt: Imperial War Museum, Lambeth, London
photo.credit: <!-- photo: imperial-war-museum-london.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "The Imperial War Museum Lambeth - geograph.org.uk - 423070" by Chris Gunns · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:The_Imperial_War_Museum_Lambeth_-_geograph.org.uk_-_423070.jpg -->

entries.name: Field Museum of Natural History
entries.desc: Chicago natural history museum on the Museum Campus, home to Sue the Tyrannosaurus rex, ancient Egyptian galleries, and wide-ranging anthropology collections.

photo.alt: Field Museum of Natural History, Chicago
photo.credit: <!-- photo: field-museum-chicago.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Field Museum of Natural History" by Joe Ravi · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Field_Museum_of_Natural_History.jpg -->

entries.name: Museum of Science and Industry
entries.desc: Science and technology museum housed in a former World's Fair building, with exhibits including a captured German U-505 submarine, a working coal mine, and a full-size aircraft.

photo.alt: Museum of Science and Industry, Chicago
photo.credit: <!-- photo: museum-of-science-and-industry-chicago.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Museum of Science and Industry (Chicago)" by zooey from Chicago, Illinois USA · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Museum_of_Science_and_Industry_(Chicago).jpg -->

entries.name: Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
entries.desc: The largest natural and cultural history museum in the western United States, known for its Dinosaur Hall, gem and mineral vault, and outdoor nature gardens.

photo.alt: Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
photo.credit: <!-- photo: natural-history-museum-los-angeles.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "NaturalHistoryMuseumOfLosAngelesCounty" by David Leigh Ellis · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:NaturalHistoryMuseumOfLosAngelesCounty.jpg -->

entries.name: American Museum of Natural History
entries.desc: Manhattan natural history complex known for its dinosaur halls, the blue whale model, dioramas of wildlife, and the adjoining Rose Center planetarium.

photo.alt: Exterior of the American Museum of Natural History in Manhattan
photo.credit: <!-- photo: american-museum-of-natural-history.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "American Museum of Natural History 02.jpg" by Gigi alt · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:American_Museum_of_Natural_History_02.jpg -->

entries.name: Intrepid Sea, Air & Space Museum
entries.desc: Military and maritime museum aboard the aircraft carrier USS Intrepid on the Hudson, displaying vintage aircraft, the submarine Growler, and the Space Shuttle Enterprise.

photo.alt: USS Intrepid docked at the Intrepid Sea, Air & Space Museum, New York City
photo.credit: <!-- photo: intrepid-sea-air-space-museum.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "USS Intrepid, Sea, Air & Space Museum, New York City (727342399).jpg" by Jim Linwood · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:USS_Intrepid,_Sea,_Air_%26_Space_Museum,_New_York_City_(727342399).jpg -->

entries.name: Exploratorium
entries.desc: Hands-on museum of science, art, and human perception on the Embarcadero, built around hundreds of interactive exhibits visitors manipulate themselves.

photo.alt: The Exploratorium building on Pier 15 along San Francisco's Embarcadero
photo.credit: <!-- photo: exploratorium-pier-15.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "San Francisco Exploratorium - South Side.jpg" by Fabrice Florin · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:San_Francisco_Exploratorium_-_South_Side.jpg -->

entries.name: California Academy of Sciences
entries.desc: Natural sciences institution in Golden Gate Park combining an aquarium, planetarium, rainforest dome, and natural history museum under one living roof.

photo.alt: The living-roof building of the California Academy of Sciences in Golden Gate Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: california-academy-of-sciences.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "California Academy of Sciences 04.JPG" by Superchilum · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:California_Academy_of_Sciences_04.JPG -->

entries.name: Smithsonian National Museum of Natural History
entries.desc: Smithsonian museum on the National Mall spanning the natural world, from dinosaur fossils and the Hope Diamond to a live insect zoo and the human origins hall.

photo.alt: The Smithsonian National Museum of Natural History on the National Mall
photo.credit: <!-- photo: smithsonian-national-museum-of-natural-history.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Smithsonian Institution National Museum of Natural History (7508870948).jpg" by Alex Proimos · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Smithsonian_Institution_National_Museum_of_Natural_History_(7508870948).jpg -->

entries.name: Smithsonian National Air and Space Museum
entries.desc: Smithsonian museum tracing the history of flight and spaceflight, with landmark craft including the Wright Flyer, the Spirit of St. Louis, and Apollo mission hardware.

photo.alt: Entrance of the Smithsonian National Air and Space Museum on the National Mall
photo.credit: <!-- photo: national-air-and-space-museum.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "National Air and Space Museum Entrance.JPG" by Jawed Karim · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:National_Air_and_Space_Museum_Entrance.JPG -->

entries.name: National Museum of American History
entries.desc: Smithsonian museum of American social, political, and cultural history, holding icons such as the original Star-Spangled Banner and the First Ladies' gowns.

photo.alt: The National Museum of American History (Kenneth E. Behring Center) on the National Mall
photo.credit: <!-- photo: national-museum-of-american-history.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "National Museum of American History- Kenneth E. Behring Center.jpg" by Kurt Kaiser · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:National_Museum_of_American_History-_Kenneth_E._Behring_Center.jpg -->

entries.name: National Museum of African American History and Culture
entries.desc: Smithsonian museum documenting African American life, history, and culture, from slavery and segregation through music, sports, and the arts across its tiered galleries.

photo.alt: The tiered bronze exterior of the National Museum of African American History and Culture
photo.credit: <!-- photo: national-museum-of-african-american-history-and-culture.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Smithsonian-nmaahc-outside-20160720.jpg" by Fuzheado · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Smithsonian-nmaahc-outside-20160720.jpg -->
